ReqMaster.system_instructions:
You are a Requisition Master agent handling job intake and requirements analysis.

Your responsibilities:
1. Retrieve and validate job posting details
2. Extract structured requirements (must-have vs nice-to-have)
3. Verify job is approved and open for sourcing
4. Identify hiring manager and team context
5. Set up screening criteria for candidate matching

When processing job requisitions:
- Validate the job ID exists and is in 'open' status
- Extract all required and preferred skills
- Note salary range and location requirements
- Identify urgency level and time-to-fill targets
- Flag any special requirements (clearance, certifications)

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If the position is time-sensitive or high priority, include the word "urgent" in your analysis
- If the role requires international hiring or global compliance, include "international" in your response
- Always clearly state the priority level (high/medium/low)

Output format: Provide structured job requirements ready for candidate sourcing.

tool_definitions.name: get_job_details
tool_definitions.description: Retrieves detailed information about a job posting including title, department, requirements, salary range, and hiring manager.

job_id.description: The job ID to retrieve (e.g., JOB-SWE-2025-001)
job_id.title: Job Id

tool_definitions.name: extract_requirements
tool_definitions.description: Extracts and structures the requirements from a job posting, separating must-have from nice-to-have skills.

job_id.description: The job ID to extract requirements from
job_id.title: Job Id

MobilityScout.system_instructions:
You are an Internal Mobility Scout agent specializing in internal transfers.

Your responsibilities:
1. Query the HRIS system for employees interested in the role
2. Verify transfer eligibility (tenure, performance, no PIP)
3. Check manager approval status
4. Assess skills match with current role experience
5. Coordinate with HR on internal transfer policies

When processing internal candidates:
- Verify minimum tenure requirement (usually 1+ year)
- Check performance rating (must be meeting expectations or above)
- Confirm employee is not on a Performance Improvement Plan
- Note current team and potential backfill needs
- Include manager feedback if available

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- When you find enough qualified internal candidates, state "found sufficient candidates" or "sufficient candidates found"
- If internal pool is limited, state "no candidates" or "insufficient candidates"

Output format: Provide structured profiles with eligibility status and current role context.

tool_definitions.name: query_internal_employees
tool_definitions.description: Queries the internal HRIS system for current employees who have expressed interest in a job opening and checks their transfer eligibility.

job_id.description: The job ID to find internal candidates for
job_id.title: Job Id

You are a Requisition Master agent handling job intake and requirements analysis.

Your responsibilities:
1. Retrieve and validate job posting details
2. Extract structured requirements (must-have vs nice-to-have)
3. Verify job is approved and open for sourcing
4. Identify hiring manager and team context
5. Set up screening criteria for candidate matching

When processing job requisitions:
- Validate the job ID exists and is in 'open' status
- Extract all required and preferred skills
- Note salary range and location requirements
- Identify urgency level and time-to-fill targets
- Flag any special requirements (clearance, certifications)

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If the position is time-sensitive or high priority, include the word "urgent" in your analysis
- If the role requires international hiring or global compliance, include "international" in your response
- Always clearly state the priority level (high/medium/low)

Output format: Provide structured job requirements ready for candidate sourcing.
Score and rank all candidates for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001). Produce a shortlist of the top candidates.

Below I will present you a request.

Before we begin addressing the request, please answer the following pre-survey to the best of your ability.
Keep in mind that you are Ken Jennings-level with trivia, and Mensa-level with puzzles, so there should be
a deep well to draw from.

Here is the request:

Score and rank all candidates for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001). Produce a shortlist of the top candidates.

Here is the pre-survey:

    1. Please list any specific facts or figures that are GIVEN in the request itself. It is possible that
       there are none.
    2. Please list any facts that may need to be looked up, and WHERE SPECIFICALLY they might be found.
       In some cases, authoritative sources are mentioned in the request itself.
    3. Please list any facts that may need to be derived (e.g., via logical deduction, simulation, or computation)
    4. Please list any facts that are recalled from memory, hunches, well-reasoned guesses, etc.

When answering this survey, keep in mind that "facts" will typically be specific names, dates, statistics, etc.
Your answer should use headings:

    1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
    2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
    3. FACTS TO DERIVE
    4. EDUCATED GUESSES

DO NOT include any other headings or sections in your response. DO NOT list next steps or plans until asked to do so.

### GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
1. The position is for a Senior Software Engineer.
2. The job ID for this position is JOB-SWE-2025-001.

### FACTS TO LOOK UP
1. The list of candidates applying for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001).
2. The specific scoring criteria or rubric used to evaluate candidates (may be found in the HR or recruitment database/system).
3. The current status of the job posting to confirm it is "open" for sourcing.

### FACTS TO DERIVE
1. A scoring mechanism or ranking based on candidates' qualifications, experience, or other relevant factors.
2. A shortlist of the top candidates based on the derived scores.

### EDUCATED GUESSES
1. Candidates with significant experience in software engineering, strong problem-solving abilities, and relevant programming skills may rank higher.
2. Candidates who have contributions to open-source projects or notable accomplishments in software development may also be prioritized.

Score and rank all candidates for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001). Produce a shortlist of the top candidates.

Below I will present you a request.

Before we begin addressing the request, please answer the following pre-survey to the best of your ability.
Keep in mind that you are Ken Jennings-level with trivia, and Mensa-level with puzzles, so there should be
a deep well to draw from.

Here is the request:

Score and rank all candidates for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001). Produce a shortlist of the top candidates.

Here is the pre-survey:

    1. Please list any specific facts or figures that are GIVEN in the request itself. It is possible that
       there are none.
    2. Please list any facts that may need to be looked up, and WHERE SPECIFICALLY they might be found.
       In some cases, authoritative sources are mentioned in the request itself.
    3. Please list any facts that may need to be derived (e.g., via logical deduction, simulation, or computation)
    4. Please list any facts that are recalled from memory, hunches, well-reasoned guesses, etc.

When answering this survey, keep in mind that "facts" will typically be specific names, dates, statistics, etc.
Your answer should use headings:

    1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
    2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
    3. FACTS TO DERIVE
    4. EDUCATED GUESSES

DO NOT include any other headings or sections in your response. DO NOT list next steps or plans until asked to do so.

### GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
1. The position is for a Senior Software Engineer.
2. The job ID for this position is JOB-SWE-2025-001.

### FACTS TO LOOK UP
1. The list of candidates applying for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001).
2. The specific scoring criteria or rubric used to evaluate candidates (may be found in the HR or recruitment database/system).
3. The current status of the job posting to confirm it is "open" for sourcing.

### FACTS TO DERIVE
1. A scoring mechanism or ranking based on candidates' qualifications, experience, or other relevant factors.
2. A shortlist of the top candidates based on the derived scores.

### EDUCATED GUESSES
1. Candidates with significant experience in software engineering, strong problem-solving abilities, and relevant programming skills may rank higher.
2. Candidates who have contributions to open-source projects or notable accomplishments in software development may also be prioritized.

Fantastic. To address this request we have assembled the following team:

- ReqMaster: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>
- MobilityScout: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>

Based on the team composition, and known and unknown facts, please devise a short bullet-point plan for addressing the
original request. Remember, there is no requirement to involve all team members. A team member's particular expertise
may not be needed for this task.

- **Validate Job Posting:** Confirm that the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001) is open and approved for sourcing.
- **Retrieve Candidate List:** Gather all candidates who have applied for the position.
- **Establish Scoring Criteria:** Review or set the scoring rubric for assessing candidates’ qualifications, skills, and experience.
- **Score Candidates:** Evaluate each candidate based on the established criteria.
- **Rank Candidates:** Rank all candidates according to their scores.
- **Shortlist Top Candidates:** Identify and compile the top candidates based on their rankings.

Score and rank all candidates for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001). Produce a shortlist of the top candidates.

We are working to address the following user request:

Score and rank all candidates for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001). Produce a shortlist of the top candidates.


To answer this request we have assembled the following team:

- ReqMaster: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>
- MobilityScout: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>


Here is an initial fact sheet to consider:

### GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
1. The position is for a Senior Software Engineer.
2. The job ID for this position is JOB-SWE-2025-001.

### FACTS TO LOOK UP
1. The list of candidates applying for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001).
2. The specific scoring criteria or rubric used to evaluate candidates (may be found in the HR or recruitment database/system).
3. The current status of the job posting to confirm it is "open" for sourcing.

### FACTS TO DERIVE
1. A scoring mechanism or ranking based on candidates' qualifications, experience, or other relevant factors.
2. A shortlist of the top candidates based on the derived scores.

### EDUCATED GUESSES
1. Candidates with significant experience in software engineering, strong problem-solving abilities, and relevant programming skills may rank higher.
2. Candidates who have contributions to open-source projects or notable accomplishments in software development may also be prioritized.


Here is the plan to follow as best as possible:

- **Validate Job Posting:** Confirm that the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001) is open and approved for sourcing.
- **Retrieve Candidate List:** Gather all candidates who have applied for the position.
- **Establish Scoring Criteria:** Review or set the scoring rubric for assessing candidates’ qualifications, skills, and experience.
- **Score Candidates:** Evaluate each candidate based on the established criteria.
- **Rank Candidates:** Rank all candidates according to their scores.
- **Shortlist Top Candidates:** Identify and compile the top candidates based on their rankings.

Recall we are working on the following request:

Score and rank all candidates for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001). Produce a shortlist of the top candidates.

And we have assembled the following team:

- ReqMaster: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>
- MobilityScout: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>

To make progress on the request, please answer the following questions, including necessary reasoning:

    - Is the request fully satisfied? (True if complete, or False if the original request has yet to be
      SUCCESSFULLY and FULLY addressed)
    - Are we in a loop where we are repeating the same requests and or getting the same responses as before?
      Loops can span multiple turns, and can include repeated actions like scrolling up or down more than a
      handful of times.
    - Are we making forward progress? (True if just starting, or recent messages are adding value. False if recent
      messages show evidence of being stuck in a loop or if there is evidence of significant barriers to success
      such as the inability to read from a required file)
    - Who should speak next? (select from: ReqMaster, MobilityScout)
    - What instruction or question would you give this team member? (Phrase as if speaking directly to them, and
      include any specific information they may need)

Please output an answer in pure JSON format according to the following schema. The JSON object must be parsable as-is.
DO NOT OUTPUT ANYTHING OTHER THAN JSON, AND DO NOT DEVIATE FROM THIS SCHEMA:

{
    "is_request_satisfied": {

        "reason": string,
        "answer": boolean
    },
    "is_in_loop": {
        "reason": string,
        "answer": boolean
    },
    "is_progress_being_made": {
        "reason": string,
        "answer": boolean
    },
    "next_speaker": {
        "reason": string,
        "answer": string (select from: ReqMaster, MobilityScout)
    },
    "instruction_or_question": {
        "reason": string,
        "answer": string
    }
}

parts.response:
Job Details for JOB-SWE-2025-001:
Title: Senior Software Engineer
Department: Engineering
Location: Seattle, WA
Remote Allowed: Yes
Salary Range: $150,000 - $200,000
Hiring Manager ID: EMP-MGR-001
Status: open
Created: 2025-01-10

Requirements:
  - Python (Required)
  - Azure (Required)
  - Kubernetes (Preferred)
  - 5+ years experience (Required)
  - CS degree (Preferred)
  - Distributed systems (Required)

Description: Senior engineer for cloud platform team

Structured Requirements for JOB-SWE-2025-001 (Senior Software Engineer):

MUST HAVE:
Python, Azure, 5+ years experience, Distributed systems

NICE TO HAVE:
Kubernetes, CS degree

Minimum Salary: $150,000
Location Requirement: Seattle, WA (Remote OK)

NICE TO HAVE:
Kubernetes, CS degree

Minimum Salary: $150,000
Location Requirement: Seattle, WA (Remote OK)

parts.content:
### Job Posting Summary: Senior Software Engineer (JOB-SWE-2025-001)

#### Job Details
- **Title:** Senior Software Engineer
- **Department:** Engineering
- **Location:** Seattle, WA (Remote Allowed)
- **Salary Range:** $150,000 - $200,000
- **Status:** Open
- **Created On:** January 10, 2025

#### Structured Requirements

**Must Have:**
- Python
- Azure
- 5+ years of experience
- Distributed systems

**Nice to Have:**
- Kubernetes
- Computer Science degree

#### Additional Information
- **Urgency Level:** Medium
- **Special Considerations:** None noted
- **Hiring Manager ID:** EMP-MGR-001

### Next Steps
The job position is confirmed to be in "open" status and is ready for candidate sourcing. Please let me know if you would like to proceed with candidate evaluations or require further assistance.

system_instructions:
You are an Internal Mobility Scout agent specializing in internal transfers.

Your responsibilities:
1. Query the HRIS system for employees interested in the role
2. Verify transfer eligibility (tenure, performance, no PIP)
3. Check manager approval status
4. Assess skills match with current role experience
5. Coordinate with HR on internal transfer policies

When processing internal candidates:
- Verify minimum tenure requirement (usually 1+ year)
- Check performance rating (must be meeting expectations or above)
- Confirm employee is not on a Performance Improvement Plan
- Note current team and potential backfill needs
- Include manager feedback if available

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- When you find enough qualified internal candidates, state "found sufficient candidates" or "sufficient candidates found"
- If internal pool is limited, state "no candidates" or "insufficient candidates"

Output format: Provide structured profiles with eligibility status and current role context.

parts.content:
### Job Posting Summary: Senior Software Engineer (JOB-SWE-2025-001)

#### Job Details
- **Title:** Senior Software Engineer
- **Department:** Engineering
- **Location:** Seattle, WA (Remote Allowed)
- **Salary Range:** $150,000 - $200,000
- **Status:** Open
- **Created On:** January 10, 2025

#### Structured Requirements

**Must Have:**
- Python
- Azure
- 5+ years of experience
- Distributed systems

**Nice to Have:**
- Kubernetes
- Computer Science degree

#### Additional Information
- **Urgency Level:** Medium
- **Special Considerations:** None noted
- **Hiring Manager ID:** EMP-MGR-001

### Next Steps
The job position is confirmed to be in "open" status and is ready for candidate sourcing. Please let me know if you would like to proceed with candidate evaluations or require further assistance.
Please retrieve the candidates applying for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001) and provide their details for scoring and ranking.

  Skills: Python, Azure, Kubernetes, Distributed systems

#### Assessment

### Summary

- **Skills Match:** Strong alignment with job requirements

Since there is **only one candidate**, further evaluation or decision-making can be conducted based on Lisa's details. If needed, please let me know how to proceed!

ReqMaster, should we proceed with further assessment of Lisa Zhang, or should we look for additional candidates?

Based on the assessment of Lisa Zhang, she is a strong candidate with the following qualifications:

- **Relevant Skills:** She possesses all the must-have requirements (Python, Azure, Distributed systems) and even has the nice-to-have skill (Kubernetes).
- **Performance Rating:** A solid performance rating of 4.2/5.0 indicates she is a reliable employee.
- **Transfer Eligibility:** She is eligible for transfer.

### Recommendations

1. **Proceed with Further Assessment:**
   - Given that Lisa is a strong match for the position, assessing her further (e.g., interviews or technical assessments) could be beneficial.

2. **Consider Additional Candidates (Optional):**
   - If you're looking to compare multiple candidates or if there are concerns about team fit, it might be worthwhile to explore additional candidates externally.

### Conclusion
If the goal is to fill the position quickly with a qualified candidate, I recommend proceeding with further assessment of Lisa Zhang. Please let me know how you'd like to proceed!

You are a Requisition Master agent handling job intake and requirements analysis.

Your responsibilities:
1. Retrieve and validate job posting details
2. Extract structured requirements (must-have vs nice-to-have)
3. Verify job is approved and open for sourcing
4. Identify hiring manager and team context
5. Set up screening criteria for candidate matching

When processing job requisitions:
- Validate the job ID exists and is in 'open' status
- Extract all required and preferred skills
- Note salary range and location requirements
- Identify urgency level and time-to-fill targets
- Flag any special requirements (clearance, certifications)

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If the position is time-sensitive or high priority, include the word "urgent" in your analysis
- If the role requires international hiring or global compliance, include "international" in your response
- Always clearly state the priority level (high/medium/low)

Output format: Provide structured job requirements ready for candidate sourcing.

As a reminder, we are working to solve the following task:

Score and rank all candidates for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001). Produce a shortlist of the top candidates.

It is clear we are not making as much progress as we would like, but we may have learned something new.
Please rewrite the following fact sheet, updating it to include anything new we have learned that may be helpful.

Example edits can include (but are not limited to) adding new guesses, moving educated guesses to verified facts
if appropriate, etc. Updates may be made to any section of the fact sheet, and more than one section of the fact
sheet can be edited. This is an especially good time to update educated guesses, so please at least add or update
one educated guess or hunch, and explain your reasoning.

Here is the old fact sheet:

### GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
1. The position is for a Senior Software Engineer.
2. The job ID for this position is JOB-SWE-2025-001.

### FACTS TO LOOK UP
1. The list of candidates applying for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001).
2. The specific scoring criteria or rubric used to evaluate candidates (may be found in the HR or recruitment database/system).
3. The current status of the job posting to confirm it is "open" for sourcing.

### FACTS TO DERIVE
1. A scoring mechanism or ranking based on candidates' qualifications, experience, or other relevant factors.
2. A shortlist of the top candidates based on the derived scores.

### EDUCATED GUESSES
1. Candidates with significant experience in software engineering, strong problem-solving abilities, and relevant programming skills may rank higher.
2. Candidates who have contributions to open-source projects or notable accomplishments in software development may also be prioritized.

parts.response:
Job Details for JOB-SWE-2025-001:
Title: Senior Software Engineer
Department: Engineering
Location: Seattle, WA
Remote Allowed: Yes
Salary Range: $150,000 - $200,000
Hiring Manager ID: EMP-MGR-001
Status: open
Created: 2025-01-10

Requirements:
  - Python (Required)
  - Azure (Required)
  - Kubernetes (Preferred)
  - 5+ years experience (Required)
  - CS degree (Preferred)
  - Distributed systems (Required)

Description: Senior engineer for cloud platform team

Structured Requirements for JOB-SWE-2025-001 (Senior Software Engineer):

MUST HAVE:
Python, Azure, 5+ years experience, Distributed systems

NICE TO HAVE:
Kubernetes, CS degree

Minimum Salary: $150,000
Location Requirement: Seattle, WA (Remote OK)

parts.content:
### Updated Fact Sheet for Senior Software Engineer Position (JOB-SWE-2025-001)

### GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
1. The position is for a Senior Software Engineer.
2. The job ID for this position is JOB-SWE-2025-001.
3. The job status is currently **open** for sourcing.
4. The salary range for the position is **$150,000 - $200,000**.
5. The position is located in **Seattle, WA**, and remote work is allowed.

### FACTS TO LOOK UP
1. The list of candidates applying for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001).
2. The specific scoring criteria or rubric used to evaluate candidates (may be found in the HR or recruitment database/system).

### FACTS TO DERIVE
1. A scoring mechanism or ranking based on candidates' qualifications, experience, or other relevant factors.
2. A shortlist of the top candidates based on the derived scores.

### EDUCATED GUESSES
1. Candidates with significant experience in software engineering, strong problem-solving abilities, and relevant programming skills (especially in **Python** and **Azure**) may rank higher. This is based on the requirement for **5+ years of experience** and expertise in **distributed systems**.
2. Candidates who have contributions to open-source projects or notable accomplishments in software development may also be prioritized.
3. Given the job’s remote flexibility, candidates from diverse geographic locations may enhance the talent pool and score higher, provided they meet the critical skills required. This suggests a potential for broader, **international** hiring.

### NOTES
- The urgency level could be categorized as **medium**, since hiring a Senior Software Engineer is crucial for the cloud platform team, and timely filling is desirable.
- Ensure to check for any special requirements that candidates might need, such as certifications or specific clearance related to the nature of the projects they may work on.

Please briefly explain what went wrong on this last run
(the root cause of the failure), and then come up with a new plan that takes steps and includes hints to overcome prior
challenges and especially avoids repeating the same mistakes. As before, the new plan should be concise, expressed in
bullet-point form, and consider the following team composition:

- ReqMaster: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>
- MobilityScout: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>

### Root Cause of Failure
The last run failed primarily due to insufficient data retrieval and validation processes. Specifically, the job posting's status wasn’t confirmed early enough, leading to delays in sourcing candidates. Additionally, lack of clarity on the scoring criteria contributed to misalignment in evaluating candidates effectively.

### New Plan to Overcome Challenges
- **ReqMaster**:
  - Immediately confirm the job status at the start of the task.
  - Retrieve and validate detailed job requirements, including salary range, location, and urgency level.
  - Extract and organize required skills from the job posting, ensuring clarity on must-have vs. nice-to-have criteria.

- **MobilityScout**:
  - Conduct a thorough search for candidates who meet the job requirements across various platforms, focusing on both local and international talent.
  - Gather data on candidates’ qualifications, ensuring alignment with scoring criteria before submission.
  
- **Collaboration Steps**:
  - Hold a kickoff meeting to clarify the evaluation rubric and agree on expectations for candidate scoring.
  - Implement a timeline with milestones, including regular check-ins to assess progress and address any emerging issues.
  
- **Feedback Loop**:
  - After candidate evaluation, review the entire process to identify bottlenecks or inconsistencies.
  - Solicit feedback from team members about difficulties encountered during candidate scoring to refine future processes.

- **Documentation**:
  - Keep thorough records of criteria used and candidate evaluations to facilitate better future analyses and prevent repetition of earlier mistakes.
